load_driver: data centers; cloud and AI campus growth
primary_bottleneck: deliverable power, transmission, generation procurement, local interconnection timing
who_pays_or_earns: regulated utility rate base if cost recovery is approved; equipment and construction providers if grid capex accelerates
public_market_handoff: D as utility exposure; ETN/PWR/VRT as equipment and infrastructure handoff candidates; CEG/VST as power-price sensitivity screens
next_evidence_needed: Dominion rate cases, PJM queue detail, county-level data-center load, generation procurement contracts
disconfirmation_metric: data-center load revisions down, faster queue clearing without capex, denied cost recovery, hyperscaler capex slowdown
workflow_note: Best first regional case because demand evidence and utility exposure are strongest.

load_driver: data centers; industrial electrification; energy-intensive compute
primary_bottleneck: transmission buildout, large-load interconnection, resource adequacy and local congestion
who_pays_or_earns: transmission owners earn through regulated investment; merchant generators benefit only if scarcity/congestion persists
public_market_handoff: SRE via Oncor ownership; VST/NRG/CEG for merchant-power sensitivity; PWR/ETN for grid buildout handoff
next_evidence_needed: ERCOT large-load interconnection queue, PUCT transmission approvals, nodal price/capacity data
disconfirmation_metric: large loads fail to materialize, congestion collapses, PUCT limits cost recovery, reserve margins improve faster than demand
workflow_note: Good for distinguishing regulated wires from merchant power; do not lump all Texas power names together.

load_driver: hyperscale campuses, manufacturing, population and industrial growth
primary_bottleneck: new generation, transmission expansion, fuel mix and regulatory approval
who_pays_or_earns: regulated utility earns only if PSC supports capex and load forecast; equipment providers earn if capex turns into orders
public_market_handoff: SO via Georgia Power; PWR/ETN and generation equipment as second-order handoff
next_evidence_needed: Georgia PSC orders, updated load forecast, customer commitments, fuel and resource plan details
disconfirmation_metric: PSC rejects load-driven capex, customer commitments weaken, cost allocation becomes politically constrained
workflow_note: Useful second trial because it tests rate-case and customer-commitment evidence.

load_driver: data centers, semiconductor fabs, population growth, cooling-sensitive load
primary_bottleneck: resource adequacy, water/cooling, solar-plus-storage integration, transmission timing
who_pays_or_earns: regulated utility capex depends on ACC approval; cooling and power-equipment vendors may have indirect exposure
public_market_handoff: PNW/APS as regulated utility screen; semiconductor/data-center operators as power-risk screens; equipment names as indirect handoff
next_evidence_needed: Arizona Corporation Commission dockets, APS/TEP load forecast, water/cooling constraints, customer load commitments
disconfirmation_metric: load forecasts are cut, ACC limits recovery, water/cooling constraints delay projects
workflow_note: Keep as watchlist until better local filings are extracted.

load_driver: data centers and cloud regions competing with grid reliability and decarbonization goals
primary_bottleneck: connection policy, grid capacity, emissions rules and political constraints
who_pays_or_earns: public-equity handoff is indirect; mainly informs policy-risk lens for data-center operators and equipment demand timing
public_market_handoff: cloud operators and European utilities only after specific connection decisions; no direct handoff yet
next_evidence_needed: specific CRU/EirGrid decision documents, connection moratorium/queue data, utility capex plans
disconfirmation_metric: connection rules ease materially, grid capacity expands faster than demand, data-center projects relocate
workflow_note: Use as policy bottleneck comparison, not a stock-selection case yet.
